table.role: Dashboard server on port 8600 — all HTML pages and /api/tasks REST endpoints
Primary language for all engine and dashboard code
Windows service manager — QI_Dashboard (port 8600), QI_HiveIngest, QI_Elevate, QI_ElevateWatchdog
Dashboard layout and component library (sidebar, cards, badges, tables)
Icon library used throughout the dashboard (bi-* classes)
Drag-and-drop kanban board — task cards draggable between columns
Custom scrollbar styling for the AdminLTE sidebar
Ecosystem intelligence backend — agents, sessions, decisions, features, snapshots
Claude Code cost tracking — parses ~/.claude/**/*.jsonl, no API required
Privilege elevation helper — 8-hour credential cache so UAC prompts happen once per workday
Autonomous service control: any Claude session drops a JSON command → broker runs it as LocalSystem
Project registry and kanban task store — file-based, no DB required for core dashboard
Source of LLM usage data — parsed by usage_stats.py for cost and token tracking

descriptions.title: Server-rendered HTML, no SPA
descriptions.body: QI Hive Dashboard renders each page as a Python function that returns an HTML string. There is no React, no Vue, no client-side routing. This is intentional: the dashboard is a development tool, not a user-facing product. Server-rendered pages are simpler to maintain, easier to debug (view-source shows the real state), and work without a JavaScript build step. AdminLTE v4 provides the visual polish via CDN CSS/JS.

descriptions.title: Elevation broker design
descriptions.body: The core problem: Claude Code sessions run as a normal user, but restarting NSSM services requires admin rights. Windows UAC prompts block autonomous loops. The solution is a three-piece system: gsudo caches admin credentials for 8 hours (one UAC per workday). The elevation broker (QI_Elevate) runs as LocalSystem via NSSM. Any Claude session drops a JSON command file into C:\QIH\commands\pending\, the broker picks it up and executes it. The whitelist regex ensures only pre-approved commands (nssm start/stop/restart for registered services) are accepted.

descriptions.title: Usage stats — no API keys required
descriptions.body: Claude Code writes a JSONL session log for every conversation at ~/.claude/projects/**/*.jsonl. Each line is a JSON object with token counts per turn and model info. usage_stats.py reads these files directly — no Anthropic API call, no authentication. It applies the public token pricing table (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku input/output rates, plus cache token multipliers) to produce per-day, per-project, and per-model cost breakdowns. Results are cached in memory for 30 seconds to keep dashboard page loads fast.

descriptions.title: QI Brain vs local fallbacks
descriptions.body: QI Brain (port 9010) is the ecosystem intelligence layer. When Brain is online, the Hive page shows real counts: sessions logged, decisions recorded, features tracked. When Brain is offline, qi_brain_client.py falls back to local inference: project count from status.json, decision proxy from session summary .docx count in the shared folder, feature proxy from tasks.json task count, session count from usage_stats.sessions_log(). The fallback numbers are reasonable approximations — not exact — until the Brain backfill is complete.
